name: GitHub Copilot CLI
summary: Terminal-first AI agent by GitHub: interactive sessions, agentic workflows, deep GitHub integration, extensibility via ACP/MCP, persistent memories, and strong tool permissioning.

features.interactive_modes: REPL-like sessions and single-shot programmatic prompts (--prompt)
features.agentic_behaviors: plan mode, autopilot, subagents/fleet, multi-step orchestration
features.github_workflows: create branches, commit, push, open PRs and issues programmatically
features.auth: GitHub OAuth, device flow, PAT support, GitHub Enterprise variants
features.permissions: trusted directory prompts, per-tool allow/deny, CLI flags (--allow-tool, --deny-tool, --allow-all-tools)
features.extensibility: ACP server, MCP registry, plugins, hooks, skills
features.lsp: Language Server Protocol integration for hovers/defs/diagnostics
features.sessions: persistent sessions, resume, rewind timeline, auto-compaction, snapshots
features.packaging: curl|bash install, Homebrew, winget, npm, tarballs

architecture.cli_first: native CLI binary orchestrating sessions, calling cloud services
architecture.acp: Agent Client Protocol server exposing sessions to IDEs/clients (WebSocket/SSE style)
architecture.mcp: Model Context Protocol servers for tool/model registries
architecture.github_api: uses GitHub API for repo/PR/issue operations
architecture.persistence: session store and memory store (likely local DB with optional cloud sync)

strengths: Deep GitHub integration for end-to-end code workflows
Granular security model and tool approval UX
weaknesses: Tight coupling to GitHub ecosystem limits portability
Plugin/marketplace requires strong sandboxing, increasing attack surface
Advanced integration features increase implementation and maintenance cost

adoptable_features.feature: Advanced tool approval CLI flags & trusted directories
adoptable_features.notes: Quick win; implement session-scoped approvals and CLI flag parsing
Server-side helper using GitHub API; integrate with permissions
SQLite memory table, heuristics, opt-in controls
Prototype minimal ACP subset via WebSocket/SSE
Start with read-only hooks, gradually expand; strong security controls
Token accounting and compaction routine; TUI timeline UI

licensing.note: Copilot CLI is a GitHub product. Check the official repo for license. Avoid copying code; implement behavior under project's license.

telemetry.privacy_notes: Provide opt-in/opt-out and retention controls; enterprise customers expect ability to disable telemetry

implementation_notes: Add CLI flags for per-tool allow/deny and persist session approvals
Implement GitHub PR helper tool in ragent-server using GitHub APIs
Prototype ACP endpoint in ragent-server with WebSocket/SSE to expose session events
Add memory table to SQLite and inject into system prompts with opt-in and retention policies

recommended_pocs.name: Tool approval flags POC
recommended_pocs.description: CLI flag parsing and session metadata persistence

recommended_pocs.name: GitHub PR helper
recommended_pocs.description: Server endpoint to create branches/PRs using GitHub API; integrate permissions

recommended_pocs.name: ACP minimal prototype
recommended_pocs.description: Expose session events over WebSocket/SSE, implement basic client handshake

recommended_pocs.name: Memory POC
recommended_pocs.description: SQLite memory store, basic read/write heuristics, injection into prompts

notes: Analysis synthesized from local COPILOTCLI_ANALYSIS.md notes, target/temp artifacts, and GitHub Docs references. Local ../copilot-cli was not present.
